problem: How many nitrogen atoms are present in 486 g of N2O5?

Which aqueous solution has the highest boiling point when 10.00 g of the substance is dissolved in 100.0 g of water?
Which compound could be used to remove Ca2+ from hard water?

Addition of silver nitrate solution to 1.0 M solutions of which compounds will result in a yellow precipitate?
I. NaCl
II. KI
A student is cleaning a buret in preparation for a titration in which the buret will be filled with 0.100 M NaOH. What should be used for the final rinse of the buret?
In which solvent does solid iodine dissolve to give a purple solution?
Which glassware would be most suitable for preparing a 0.1500 M solution of silver nitrate?

A 2.00 g sample of NaHCO3(s) is heated in a 10.00 g crucible to drive off H2O and CO2. The mass data in the table were collected by heating the crucible and then periodically letting it cool to room temperature and reweighing it. Which is the first measurement after which heating could have been stopped?

Measurement 1: Mass of crucible and solid = 11.48 g
Measurement 2: Mass of crucible and solid = 11.24 g
Measurement 3: Mass of crucible and solid = 11.25 g
Measurement 4: Mass of crucible and solid = 11.24 g
1.53 g of a gas occupies a volume of 264 mL at 722 mm Hg and 22 °C. What is the molecular formula of the gas?
A mole of which gas will have a volume closest to 22.4 L at 0 °C and 1 atm pressure?

Two identical steel cylinders at the same temperature contain equal masses of CO(g) and C2H2(g). Which quantities will be greater for the cylinder containing C2H2?
I. Pressure
II. Average molecular velocity
The critical point of carbon dioxide is 31.0 °C and 72.8 atm. Which best describes the composition of a sample of pure CO2 at 29.0 °C and 75 atm?
choices: A mixture of gaseous CO2 and liquid CO2 is present.

problem: The normal boiling point of ethane (C2H6) is -88.5 °C, while the normal boiling point of fluoromethane (CH3F) is -78.4 °C. A difference in which type of intermolecular forces is most responsible for the higher boiling point of fluoromethane?
Cadmium metal crystallizes in a hexagonal closest packed structure. How many nearest neighbors does a cadmium atom have in this structure?
The standard enthalpy of fusion of 1,4-dichlorobenzene is 18.2 kJ mol-1 and its standard entropy of fusion is 55.3 J mol-1 K-1. What is the melting point of 1,4-dichlorobenzene?

What is ΔH°rxn for the gas-phase reaction of ethene and fluorine shown below?
C2H4(g) + 6 F2(g) → 2 CF4(g) + 4 HF(g)

Substance | ΔH°f (kJ mol-1)
C2H4(g) | 52.4
CF4(g) | -679.0
HF(g) | -273.3

Which reactions are spontaneous under standard conditions?
I. N2H4(l) → N2(g) + 2 H2(g), Kp = 1.4 × 10^26
II. MgCO3(s) → MgO(s) + CO2(g), Kp = 7.0 × 10^-9

Solid gallium melts at 29.8 °C with an enthalpy of fusion of 5.59 kJ mol-1. The heat capacity of solid gallium is 26.1 J mol-1 K-1 and the heat capacity of liquid gallium is 28.5 J mol-1 K-1. If equal masses of solid gallium at 20.0 °C and liquid gallium at 60.0 °C are mixed in a well-insulated container, what is the temperature after the mixture has attained equilibrium?

What is the Ksp of AgBr at 298 K?

Species | ΔG°f (298 K) (kJ mol-1)
Ag+(aq) | 77.1
Br-(aq) | -102.8
AgBr(s) | -96.9

For a chemical reaction under conditions where it is at equilibrium, which statements must be true?
I. ΔSrxn > 0
II. ΔGrxn = 0

When iodate ion reacts with iodide ion in acidic solution, which statement about how concentrations are changing is correct?
IO3-(aq) + 8 I-(aq) + 6 H+(aq) → 3 I3-(aq) + 3 H2O(l)

For the irreversible reaction between CO(g) and Cl2(g) to form COCl2(g), the rate law is Rate = k[CO][Cl2]. In an experiment where equimolar quantities of CO(g) and Cl2(g) are reacted, which of the following would change linearly with reaction time?

A sample containing radium-226 was measured in 1925 to have an activity of 3.74 × 10^6 disintegrations per second. The same sample, re-measured in 2025, has an activity of 3.58 × 10^6 disintegrations per second. What is the half-life of 226Ra?

The rate constant for the addition of pyridine to an iridium compound is measured at a variety of temperatures, and the variation of ln(k) with 1/T is shown below. What is the activation energy of this reaction?

cyclo-C3H6 ⇌(k1/k-1) •CH2CH2CH2• →(k2) CH3CH=CH2

The reaction of MnO4-(aq) and H2C2O4(aq) in acidic solution is autocatalytic because one of the products, Mn2+(aq), is a reducing agent. Starting from a mixture of reactants, which of the following graphs best shows how the concentration of MnO4-(aq) changes over time?
choices: Sigmoidal decrease (slow initially, then fast, then leveling off)

problem:
Which statements must be correct at equilibrium?
I. The rate constants for the forward and reverse reactions are equal.
II. The rates of the forward and reverse reactions are equal.

AgCl(s) + 2 NH3(aq) ⇌ Ag(NH3)2+(aq) + Cl-(aq)

A buffer is made by mixing 0.900 mol Na[CH3CO2] and 0.500 mol HCl in enough water to make 2.50 L of solution. What is the pH of this solution? The pKa of CH3COOH is 4.75.

A sealed container with a volume of 1.00 L contains an equilibrium mixture of N2O4(g) and NO2(g) at a given temperature. Which quantities will increase if the volume of the container is increased to 2.00 L while maintaining the same temperature?
I. The number of moles of NO2(g)
II. The partial pressure of NO2(g)

A solution is 0.05 M in each of the anions fluoride (F-), carbonate (CO3^2-), and phosphate (PO4^3-). As calcium ion is added slowly to this solution, in what order will the solids precipitate? The Ksp values are: CaF2, 3.9 × 10^-11; CaCO3, 3.8 × 10^-9; Ca3(PO4)2, 1.0 × 10^-25.
choices: First CaF2, then CaCO3, then Ca3(PO4)2
First CaF2, then Ca3(PO4)2, then CaCO3
First Ca3(PO4)2, then CaF2, then CaCO3
First Ca3(PO4)2, then CaCO3, then CaF2

I. Ionization energy

choices: Trigonal pyramidal with lone pair on S

choices: All six carbon atoms are in the same plane.
All hydrogens are eclipsed relative to the hydrogen atoms on adjoining carbons.
The carbon-carbon bonds alternate between short and long distances.

problem: The tertiary alcohol 2-methyl-2-propanol reacts with concentrated aqueous hydrochloric acid at 0 °C to form 2-chloro-2-methylpropane. How is the mechanism of this reaction best described?

choices: Ketones are weak acids; esters are strong acids.
